title: Bridging the Global Digital Divide for Women and Girls through Multilateral Diplomacy
summary: On March 23, 2022, the Future of Diplomacy Project, the Center for Public Leadership, and the Women in Public Policy Program hosted a conversation with Dr. Phumzile Mlambo-Ngcuka, former Executive Director of UN Women, on the role of education in improving outcomes for women and girls worldwide and how multilateral organizations, national governments, and private companies must work together to expand access to technology and close the digital divide. Erika Manouselis, Research and Administrative Manager at the Future of Diplomacy Project, moderated this discussion.
body: On March 23, 2022, the Future of Diplomacy Project, the Center for Public Leadership, and the Women in Public Policy Program hosted a conversation with Dr. Phumzile Mlambo-Ngcuka , former Executive Director of UN Women, on the role of education in improving outcomes for women and girls worldwide and how multilateral organizations, national governments, and private companies must work together to expand access to technology and close the digital divide. Erika Manouselis, Research and Administrative Manager at the Future of Diplomacy Project, moderated this discussion. _list.source: Belfer Center for Science and International Affairs

result.main_content_en: On March 23, 2022, the Future of Diplomacy Project, alongside the Center for Public Leadership and the Women in Public Policy Program, hosted a discussion featuring Dr. Phumzile Mlambo-Ngcuka, former Executive Director of UN Women. The conversation focused on the critical role of education in advancing outcomes for women and girls globally. It emphasized the necessity for multilateral organizations, national governments, and private sector companies to collaborate in expanding technological access and bridging the digital divide. The event was moderated by Erika Manouselis, Research and Administrative Manager at the Future of Diplomacy Project, highlighting the intersection of diplomacy, gender equity, and technology policy as a means for inclusive global development.
result.main_content_zh: 2022年3月23日，未来外交项目、公共领导力中心及公共政策女性项目共同举办了一场讨论会，特邀联合国妇女署前执行主任普姆齐莱·姆兰博-努卡博士参与。对话核心探讨了教育在改善全球妇女和女童发展成果中的关键作用。会议强调，多边组织、各国政府及私营企业必须通力合作，以扩大技术获取途径并弥合数字鸿沟。此次讨论由未来外交项目研究与行政经理埃里卡·马诺塞利斯主持，凸显了将外交、性别平等与技术政策相结合，作为推动包容性全球发展的重要手段。
